content:
# Decision: Codebase-to-Course as Separate Initiative with Adapter-Per-Signal Architecture

## Context

Research into extracting "why" from codebases (March 2026) revealed no tool combines all four signal sources (code structure, git history, PR/issue context, LLM synthesis). Corvia is positioned to be the first. The work was initially considered as a reframe of P6 under Structural Intelligence but is large enough to stand alone.

## Decision

1. **Separate initiative, not P6.** Codebase-to-course self-onboarding is its own workstream, not a sub-item of the Structural Intelligence roadmap. P1-P5 structural intelligence feeds into it but is independently scoped.

2. **One adapter per signal source.** Each knowledge signal is its own adapter, following corvia's existing adapter architecture. Adapters can be added, removed, or swapped without affecting others.

   | Signal | Adapter | Status |
   |--------|---------|--------|
   | Code structure (AST, imports, calls) | `adapter-git` | Exists |
   | Git temporal history (commits, blame, diffs, co-change) | `adapter-git-history` (new) | Not started |
   | PR/issue discussion (descriptions, reviews, linked issues) | `adapter-github` (new, already planned in D44) | Not started |
   | LLM synthesis (orchestration layer consuming all signals) | Kernel-level, not an adapter | Not started |

3. **Benchmark each signal's contribution independently.** When a new adapter lands, measure its impact on synthesis quality before adding the next. Signals that don't improve understanding get removed or deprioritized.

4. **Incremental delivery.** Ship adapter-by-adapter. Each adapter produces knowledge entries + edges that are immediately useful for search/retrieval even before the synthesis layer exists.

## Rationale

- Matches corvia's design philosophy: flexibility and compatibility over monolithic features
- Each adapter is independently testable and shippable
- Benchmarking per-signal avoids the "kitchen sink" problem where you can't tell what's helping
- adapter-git-history and adapter-github produce valuable knowledge entries on their own (temporal patterns, PR context), independent of the synthesis step
- The synthesis/course layer becomes a consumer of whatever adapters are available, gracefully degrading when signals are missing

## Relationship to Structural Intelligence

P1-P5 structural intelligence improves Signal 1 (code structure) quality. Better edges from tree-sitter extraction directly improve the synthesis layer's module grouping and relationship understanding. But P1-P5 ships on its own timeline. The codebase-to-course initiative consumes whatever structural edges exist at the time.
